title: alyssum montanum subsp. scardicum
intro: Alyssum montanum subsp. scardicum is a subspecies of the alpine alyssum plant, a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Alyssum montanum subsp. scardicum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to soothe digestive problems. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
